case_title: WENCESLAO CALDERON VS. UNITED SITE SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC., A ET AL

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HUGO SANCHEZ AND LUIS SALAS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT: HUGO SANCHEZ AND LOUIS SALAS' DEMURRER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A DEMURRER IS FILED. A DECLARATION MUST THEN BE FILED WHICH SPECIFIES WHETHER THE MEET AND CONFER WAS IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY NOVEMBER 8, 2022. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JORGE H. REYES, Esq. of Reyes & Associates, P.C. (Tel: 213-383-6244) for plaintiff;
Atty. MICHAEL A.S. NEWMAN, Esq. of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP (Tel: 213-680-2800) for defendants United Site Services of California, Inc., Hugo Sanchez, and Luis Salas.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UNITED SITE SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'s Motion For Deemed Admissions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court vacates its tentative ruling, and rules as follows:

Motion is denied because the opposing party served substantially-compliant responses prior to the hearing. This does not preclude the moving party from filing a motion to compel further responses. Monetary sanctions are awarded against the plaintiff in the amount of $769.50 to be paid by the plaintiff within 20 days.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order in Word format to the pro tem judge at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-22-2022 Continued To Feb-07-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jorge Reyes, Esq. (213.383.6244; jhreyes@reyeslawpc.com) of Reyes & Associates, PC for Plaintiff.
Michael Newman, Esq. (213.680.2800; mnewman@hinshawlaw.com) of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS HUGO SANCHEZ and LUIS SALAS' DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT:

Hugo Sanchez and Louis Salas' demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed. A declaration must then be filed which specifies whether the meet and confer was in person or by telephone. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and Defendant's response to the first amended complaint is now due by November 8, 2022.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
